revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:庚申社]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[ファイル:Koushin001.JPG|thumb|right|200px|拝殿]] '''庚申社'''（こうしんしゃ）は[[福岡県]][[直方市]]山部にある[[神仏習合]]で祀る[[神社]]。
[[境内]]には[[庚申]]の「申」と[[猿田彦大神]]の「猿」を結び付けられたものと考えられ、[[庚申信仰]]の[[三尸]]（さんし）説「見ざる聞かざる云わざる」等 の石で彫られた「さる」が数百体ある。
== 祭神 == *[[神道]]では[[猿田彦大神]] *[[仏教]]では[[青面金剛]] == 由緒 == [[猿田彦大神]]を主神として元文貳年貳月(1737年)に唐津の庚申神社より現在地に鎮座した。 [[ファイル:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|三猿]] == 行事・祭典 == *12月5日:例大祭 *8月5日:夏越祭 *毎月5日:月次祭(御神徳にあやかり1959年から直方中心商店街で「五日市」と呼ばれる大売出しのイベントが開催されている。) == 所在地・交通 == 福岡県直方市山部601 *JR直方駅より徒歩10分 ==関連項目== *[[庚申]] *[[庚申待]] *[[庚申信仰]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp 庚申社](公式サイト)
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:直方市]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[ファイル:Koushin001.JPG|thumb|right|200px|拝殿]] '''庚申社'''（こうしんしゃ）は[[福岡県]][[直方市]]山部にある[[神仏習合]]で祀る[[神社]]。
[[境内]]には[[庚申]]の「申」と[[猿田彦大神]]の「猿」を結び付けられたものと考えられ、[[庚申信仰]]の[[三尸]]（さんし）説「見ざる聞かざる云わざる」等 の石で彫られた「さる」が数百体ある。
== 祭神 == *[[神道]]では[[猿田彦大神]] *[[仏教]]では[[青面金剛]] == 由緒 == [[猿田彦大神]]を主神として元文貳年貳月(1737年)に唐津の庚申神社より現在地に鎮座した。 [[ファイル:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|三猿]] == 行事・祭典 == *12月5日:例大祭 *8月5日:夏越祭 *毎月5日:月次祭(御神徳にあやかり1959年から直方中心商店街で「五日市」と呼ばれる大売出しのイベントが開催されている。) == 所在地・交通 == 福岡県直方市山部601 *JR直方駅より徒歩10分 ==関連項目== *[[庚申]] *[[庚申待]] *[[庚申信仰]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp 庚申社](公式サイト)
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:直方市]] [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:神仏習合]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 祭神 == *[[神道]]では[[猿田彦大神]] *[[仏教]]では[[青面金剛]] == 由緒 == [[猿田彦大神]]を主神として元文貳年貳月(1737年)に唐津の庚申神社より現在地に鎮座した。

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 庚申社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[ファイル:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
拝殿 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[福岡県]][[直方市]][[山部 (直方市)|山部]]601 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}}| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[猿田彦大神]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }} '''庚申社'''（こうしんしゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[直方市]][[山部 (直方市)|山部]]もと[[鞍手郡]][[山部村 (福岡県)|山部村]]にある[[神仏習合]]で祀る[[神社]]。 [[境内]]には[[庚申]]の「申」と[[猿田彦大神]]の「猿」を結び付けられたものと考えられ、[[庚申信仰]]の[[三尸]]（さんし）説「見ざる聞かざる云わざる」などの石で彫られた「さる」が数百体ある。 == 祭神 == * [[神道]]では[[猿田彦大神]] * [[仏教]]では[[青面金剛]] == 由緒 == [[猿田彦大神]]を主神として[[元文]]2年（[[1737年]]）2月に[[唐津市|唐津]]の[[庚申神社]]より現在地に鎮座した。 == 行事・祭典 == [[ファイル:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|三猿]] * 12月5日: 例大祭 * 8月5日: 夏越祭 * 毎月5日: 月次祭(御神徳にあやかり1959年から直方中心商店街で「五日市」と呼ばれる大売出しのイベントが開催されている。) == 所在地・交通 == : 福岡県直方市山部601 * JR直方駅より徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[庚申堂]] * [[庚申信仰]] * [[庚申待]] * [[庚申]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp 庚申社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf 五日市]（市報のおがた） {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の寺]] [[Category:直方市の建築物]] [[Category:直方市の歴史]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:庚申社]][[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:庚申社]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:庚申社]][[Category:qq]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=福岡県}}

(福岡県)|山部村]]にある[[神仏習合]]で祀る[[神社]]。 [[境内]]には[[庚申]]の「申」と[[猿田彦大神]]の「猿」を結び付けられたものと考えられ、[[庚申信仰]]の[[三尸]]（さんし）説「見ざる聞かざる云わざる」などの石で彫られた「さる」が数百体ある。 == 祭神 == * [[神道]]では[[猿田彦大神]] * [[仏教]]では[[青面金剛]] == 由緒 == [[猿田彦大神]]を主神として[[元文]]2年（[[1737年]]）2月に[[唐津市|唐津]]の[[庚申神社]]より現在地に鎮座した。 == 行事・祭典 == [[ファイル:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|三猿]] * 12月5日: 例大祭 * 8月5日: 夏越祭 * 毎月5日: 月次祭(御神徳にあやかり1959年から直方中心商店街で「五日市」と呼ばれる大売出しのイベントが開催されている。) == 所在地・交通 == : 福岡県直方市山部601 * JR直方駅より徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[庚申堂]] * [[庚申信仰]] * [[庚申待]] * [[庚申]] == 外部リンク == * {{神社 |名称 = 庚申社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[ファイル:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
拝殿 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[福岡県]][[直方市]][[山部 (直方市)|山部]]601 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}}| | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[猿田彦大神]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }} '''庚申社'''（こうしんしゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[直方市]][[山部 (直方市)|山部]]もと[[鞍手郡]][[山部村 (福岡県)|山部村]]にある[[神仏習合]]で祀る[[神社]]。 [[境内]]には[[庚申]]の「申」と[[猿田彦大神]]の「猿」を結び付けられたものと考えられ、[[庚申信仰]]の[[三尸]]（さんし）説「見ざる聞かざる云わざる」などの石で彫られた「さる」が数百体ある。 == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * [[神道]]では[[猿田彦大神]] * [[仏教]]では[[青面金剛]] == 由緒 == [[猿田彦大神]]を主神として[[元文]]2年（[[1737年]]）2月に[[唐津市|唐津]]の[[庚申神社]]より現在地に鎮座した。 == 行事・祭典 == [[ファイル:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|三猿]] * 12月5日: 例大祭 * 8月5日: 夏越祭 * 毎月5日: 月次祭(御神徳にあやかり1959年から直方中心商店街で「五日市」と呼ばれる大売出しのイベントが開催されている。) == 所在地・交通 == : 福岡県直方市山部601 * JR直方駅より徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[Kōshin-dō]] * [[庚申信仰]] * [[庚申待]] * [[庚申]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp 庚申社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf 五日市]（市報のおがた） {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の寺]] [[Category:直方市の建築物]] [[Category:直方市の歴史]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:庚申社}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{神社 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}} {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)}} 601 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} | type = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}}, in the Yamabe areaもと{{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)}}. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a syncretic (【神仏習合】) tradition. Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Koushin faith|ja|庚申信仰}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * In {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}. * In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}}. == History (Yūshi) == With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737). == Festivals and Events == [[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]] * December 5: Annual Grand Festival * August 5: Natsugoe Festival * Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues). == Location and Transportation == : {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601 * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR Nogata Station|ja|直方駅}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}} * {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}} * {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待}} * {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} == External Links == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}}] (Official Site) * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper) {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:{{ill|Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の神社}}]] [[Category:{{ill|Temples in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の寺}}]] [[Category:{{ill|Buildings in Nogata City|ja|直方市の建築物}}]] [[Category:{{ill|History of Nogata City|ja|直方市の歴史}}]] [[Category:{{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}]] {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の寺]] [[Category:直方市の建築物]] [[Category:直方市の歴史]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:Kōshin]] {{神社 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}} {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)}} 601 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} | type = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}}, in the Yamabe areaもと{{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)}}. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a syncretic (【神仏習合】) tradition. Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Koushin faith|ja|庚申信仰}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * In {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}. * In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}}. == History (Yūshi) == With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737). == Festivals and Events == [[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]] * December 5: Annual Grand Festival * August 5: Natsugoe Festival * Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues). == Location and Transportation == : {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601 * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR Nogata Station|ja|直方駅}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}} * {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}} * {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待}} * {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} == External Links == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}}] (Official Site) * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper) {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:ill|Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の神社}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Buildings in Nogata City|ja|直方市の建築物}}]] [[Category:ill|History of Nogata City|ja|直方市の歴史}}]] [[Category:ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}]] {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の寺]] [[Category:直方市の建築物]] [[Category:直方市の歴史]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:Kōshin]] {{神社 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}} {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)}} 601 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} | type = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}}, in the Yamabe areaもと{{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)}}. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a syncretic (【神仏習合】) tradition. Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Koushin faith|ja|庚申信仰}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * In {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}. * In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}}. == History (Yūshi) == With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737). == Festivals and Events == [[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]] * December 5: Annual Grand Festival * August 5: Natsugoe Festival * Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues). == Location and Transportation == : {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601 * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR Nogata Station|ja|直方駅}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}} * {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}} * {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待}} * {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} == External Links == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}}] (Official Site) * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper) {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:ill|Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の神社}}]] {{神社 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}} {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)}} 601 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} | type = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}}, in the Yamabe areaもと{{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)}}. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a syncretic (【神仏習合】) tradition. Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Koushin faith|ja|庚申信仰}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * In {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}. * In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}}. == History (Yūshi) == With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737). == Festivals and Events == [[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]] * December 5: Annual Grand Festival * August 5: Natsugoe Festival * Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues). == Location and Transportation == : {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601 * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR Nogata Station|ja|直方駅}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}} * {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}} * {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待}} * {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} == External Links == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}}] (Official Site) * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper) {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:ill|Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の神社}}]] {{神社 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}} {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)}} 601 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} | type = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市}}, in the Yamabe areaもと{{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)}}. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a syncretic (【神仏習合】) tradition. Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Koushin faith|ja|庚申信仰}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * In {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}. * In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}}. == History (Yūshi) == With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737). == Festivals and Events == [[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]] * December 5: Annual Grand Festival * August 5: Natsugoe Festival * Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues). == Location and Transportation == : {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601 * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR Nogata Station|ja|直方駅}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}} * {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}} * {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待}} * {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} == External Links == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社}}] (Official Site) * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper) {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:ill|Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の神社}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples in Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Buildings in Nogata City|ja|直方市の建築物}}]] [[Category:ill|History of Nogata City|ja|直方市の歴史}}]] [[Category:ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}]] {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の寺]] [[Category:直方市の建築物]] [[Category:直方市の歴史]] [[Category:神仏習合]] revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)|12=draft}} 601 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}} | type = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市|12=draft}}, in the Yamabe areaもと{{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=draft}}. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a syncretic (【神仏習合】) tradition. Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Koushin faith|ja|庚申信仰|12=draft}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * In {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教|12=draft}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=draft}}. == History (Yūshi) == With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市|12=draft}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737). == Festivals and Events == [[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]] * December 5: Annual Grand Festival * August 5: Natsugoe Festival * Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues). == Location and Transportation == : {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601 * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR Nogata Station|ja|直方駅|12=draft}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社|12=draft}}] (Official Site) * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper) {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=福岡県}} {{draft categories| {{神社 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)|12=draft}} 601 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}} | type = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nogata City|ja|直方市|12=draft}}, in the Yamabe areaもと{{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=draft}}. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a syncretic (【神仏習合】) tradition. Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Koushin faith|ja|庚申信仰|12=draft}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * In {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教|12=draft}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=draft}}. == History (Yūshi) == With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市|12=draft}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737). == Festivals and Events == [[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]] * December 5: Annual Grand Festival * August 5: Natsugoe Festival * Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues). == Location and Transportation == : {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601 * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR Nogata Station|ja|直方駅|12=draft}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社|12=draft}}] (Official Site) * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper) {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=福岡県}} {{draft categories| 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:庚申社 up to rev 63757901
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 庚申社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Koushin001.JPG|240px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[直方市]][[山部 (直方市)|山部]]601 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}}| |祭神 = [[猿田彦大神]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''庚申社'''（こうしんしゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[直方市]][[山部 (直方市)|山部]]もと[[鞍手郡]][[山部村 (福岡県)|山部村]]にある[[神仏習合]]で祀る[[神社]]。 [[境内]]には[[庚申]]の「申」と[[猿田彦大神]]の「猿」を結び付けられたものと考えられ、[[庚申信仰]]の[[三尸]]（さんし）説「見ざる聞かざる云わざる」などの石で彫られた「さる」が数百体ある。 == 祭神 == * [[神道]]では[[猿田彦大神]] * [[仏教]]では[[青面金剛]] == 由緒 == [[猿田彦大神]]を主神として[[元文]]2年（[[1737年]]）2月に[[唐津市|唐津]]の[[庚申神社]]より現在地に鎮座した。 == 行事・祭典 == [[ファイル:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|三猿]] * 12月5日: 例大祭 * 8月5日: 夏越祭 * 毎月5日: 月次祭(御神徳にあやかり1959年から直方中心商店街で「五日市」と呼ばれる大売出しのイベントが開催されている。) == 所在地・交通 == : 福岡県直方市山部601 * JR直方駅より徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[庚申堂]] * [[庚申信仰]] * [[庚申待]] * [[庚申]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp 庚申社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf 五日市]（市報のおがた） {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の寺]] [[Category:直方市の建築物]] [[Category:直方市の歴史]] [[Category:庚申信仰]] [[Category:神仏習合]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=福岡県}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building
 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushinsha|qq=|1=Kōshin-sha (Nōgata, Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486003}}
 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]
 | image = File:Koushin001.JPG
 | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata City]] {{ill|Yamabe|ja|山部 (直方市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 601
 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037}}
 | type = 
 | 創建 = 
 | 本殿 = 
 | 別名 = 
 | 札所等 = 
 | festival = 
 | 神事 = 
| location = {{ill|Nogata|qid=Q861296}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|33.744642|N|130.723592|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 }}
'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata City]], in the Yamabe area<ref>もと[[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]] {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of [[:en:Kōshin|Koushin faith]], such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|qq=|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]] in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Nōgata Station|JR Nogata Station]].

== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Kōshin|Koushin Faith]]
* {{ill|Koushin Machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushin Machi|qq=|1=Kōshin-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486004}}
* {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

== External Links ==
* [http://www.koushinsha.tsurugijinja.jp] (Official Site)
* [http://www.city.nogata.fukuoka.jp/var/rev0/0007/8941/210301shihou_P02-03.pdf Gokichi] (Nogata City Newspaper)

{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}

* December 5: Annual Grand Festival

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of [[:en:Kōshin|Koushin faith]], such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]] in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Nōgata Station|JR Nogata Station]].

'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata City]], in the Yamabe area<ref>もと[[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]] {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of [[:en:Kōshin|Koushin faith]], such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]] in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Nōgata Station|JR Nogata Station]].

'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata City]], in the Yamabe area<ref>もと[[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]] {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of [[:en:Kōshin|Koushin faith]], such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]] in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Nōgata Station|JR Nogata Station]].

'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata City]], in the Yamabe area<ref>もと[[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]] {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of [[:en:Kōshin|Koushin faith]], such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]] in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Nōgata Station|JR Nogata Station]].

{{Reflist}}

* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=JR Nogata Station}}.

'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nogata City}}, in the Yamabe area<ref>もと{{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|WD=Q3082157|lt=Koushin faith}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=JR Nogata Station}}.

'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nogata City|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}, in the Yamabe area<ref>もと{{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District|1=Kurate District, Fukuoka}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|WD=Q3082157|lt=Koushin faith|1=Kōshin}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu|1=Karatsu, Saga}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=JR Nogata Station|1=Nōgata Station}}.

{{Infobox religious building
 | 名称 = {{ill|Koushinsha|ja|庚申社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushinsha|1=Kōshin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486003}}

 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|44|40.71|N|130|43|24.93|E|scale:30000}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}

 }}
'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nogata City|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}, in the Yamabe area<ref>もと{{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District|1=Kurate District, Fukuoka}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|WD=Q3082157|lt=Koushin faith|1=Kōshin}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu|1=Karatsu, Saga}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=JR Nogata Station|1=Nōgata Station}}.

'''Koushinsha''' (こうしんしゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|qid=Q861296|lt=Nogata City|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}, in the Yamabe area<ref>もと{{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District|1=Kurate District, Fukuoka}} {{ill|Yamabe Village|ja|山部村 (福岡県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>. It is a shrine that enshrines deities in a [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] tradition.

Within the shrine grounds, stone-carved figures of monkeys—linking the character "申" in "Koushin" with the "猿" (monkey) of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}—are believed to represent the "Mitsushi" (three corpses) theory of {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|qid=Q3082157|lt=Koushin faith|1=Kōshin}}, such as the "see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil" concept, with several hundred carved "saru" present.

== Worshipped Deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* In {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}, the deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}.
* In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, the deity is {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662826}}.

== History (Yūshi) ==
With {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} as the principal deity, the shrine was transferred to its present location from the Koushin Shrine in {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|qid=Q738275|lt=Karatsu|1=Karatsu, Saga}} in February of Genbun 2 (1737).

== Festivals and Events ==
[[File:Koushin002.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Three monkeys]]
* December 5: Annual Grand Festival
* August 5: Natsugoe Festival
* Every month on the 5th: A monthly festival is held (since 1959, a large sale event called "Itsukaichi" has been organized in the central shopping district of Nogata, capitalizing on the divine virtues).

== Location and Transportation ==
: {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} Nogata City, Yamabe 601
* A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|qid=Q7071610|lt=JR Nogata Station|1=Nōgata Station}}.
